data.narrative:
Xage Security is a Series B defense and security company that has raised $80M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a comparable universe of 10 peers—2 public (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS) and 8 private companies—all operating in the Defense & Security sector at similar stages. The private peer set includes Vannevar Labs, Dream Security, Harmattan AI, Castelion, and Defense Unicorns.

Two triangulation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis uses the $80M total raised as a proxy base value and applies a 1.9x step-up multiplier typical for Series B advancement, yielding $152M. Peer-median valuation captures the median across 6 same-sector, same-stage private comparables at $1.2B, with a range of $1.0B to $1.5B. The peer set exhibits meaningful dispersion, with valuations spanning from Castelion and Defense Unicorns at $1.0B each to Vannevar Labs and Dream Security at $1.5B.

The two-method triangulation implies a valuation range of $129M (low) to $1.4B (high), with a median of $676M. The wide spread reflects material uncertainty around Xage's positioning relative to better-capitalized peers and revenue/growth comparability. Stage-progression suggests relative undervaluation versus peers, whereas peer-median implies substantial premium to capital deployed. Key uncertainties include customer concentration, revenue scale relative to comparable companies, and defensibility of technology in an increasingly crowded defense-tech market.
